title: pyrus pompejana
intro: Pyrus pompejana, also known as the Korean mountain ash, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Pyrus pompejana have been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness their medicinal properties.
